title: Minor syntax bug in verilator generated VCD file
body:
---


Author Name: **Jonathon Donaldson**
Original Redmine Issue: 808 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2014-08-18


---

I am using verilator with the -sc switch and creating my testbench in SystemC. The design is working very well and I've had no problems...until now.

I just recently add the --trace option to the verilator command and it successfully generates the VCD file which contains the correct sample information. However, when I attempt to open the VCD file in gtkwave I get the following error:

```GTKWave Analyzer v3.3.34 (w)1999-2012 BSI

Error opening  .vcd file 'obj_dir/trace.vcd'.
```

This message provided me with absolutely no useful information so it took me a while but I finally figured out what the problem was by comparing the VCD with a number of other working VCD files.

The problem was right in the first few lines of the file which I have posted below.

```
$version Generated by VerilatedVcd $end
$date Sun Aug 17 20:07:57 2014
 $end
$timescale 1ps $end

 $scope module My_Testbench.My_Counter $end
  $var wire  1 ) i_clk $end
  $var wire  1 * i_load $end
  $var wire  8 + i_lval [7:0] $end
  $var wire  1 ( i_rst $end
  $var wire  8 , o_cnt [7:0] $end
  $scope module v $end
    $var wire  1 $ i_clk $end
    $var wire  1 % i_load $end
    $var wire  8 & i_lval [7:0] $end
    $var wire  1 # i_rst $end
    $var wire  8 ' o_cnt [7:0] $end
  $upscope $end
 $upscope $end
$enddefinitions $end

```

The problem lies in the "My_Testbench.My_Counter". For whatever reason GTKwave does not like the '.' (period) character. I simply changed the period to an underscore and all is well.

If it's useful to know, "My_Counter" is the verilator generated SC_MODULE created from my systemverilog source, and "My_Testbench" is a structural SC_MODULE which instances the verilated My_Counter SC_MODULE and my counter_stim SC_MODULE. Hierarchically this can be represented as:

```
Top-Level SC_MODULE "My_Testbench"
==> instances SC_MODULE "My_Counter"
==> instances SC_MODULE "My_Counter_Stim"

```

Let me know if you have any other questions.
